subject: ap-precalculus
title: Sinusoidal Modeling: Tidal Depth & Polar Curve Analysis
stimulus: The depth of water at the entrance to a harbor changes sinusoidally with time due to tides. At time $t = 0$ hours, the depth is at the midline value of $8$ meters and decreasing. The amplitude of the tidal variation is $3$ meters, and the period is $12.4$ hours.

parts.prompt:
The depth $D$ of the water in meters at time $t$ hours can be written as $D(t) = a\sin(b(t - c)) + d$.

(i) Find values of $a$, $b$, $c$, and $d$.

(ii) The graph of $D$ and its dashed midline for one full cycle is shown, with five labeled points $A$, $B$, $C$, $E$, $F$ starting at the midline (decreasing) and going through one complete cycle. No scale is indicated. Determine possible coordinates $(t, D(t))$ for these five points.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct parameter values — $a = -3$ (amplitude $= 3$, negative because function is decreasing at $t = 0$), $d = 8$ (midline), $b = \frac{2\pi}{12.4} = \frac{\pi}{6.2}$, $c = 0$ (starts at midline with downward motion, consistent with $-3\sin(bt)$); accept $a = 3$ with $c = 6.2$ (quarter-period phase shift from minimum); so $D(t) = -3\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) + 8$

1 pt: Correct coordinates — $A = (0, 8)$, $B = (3.1, 5)$, $C = (6.2, 8)$, $E = (9.3, 11)$, $F = (12.4, 8)$; $t$-coordinates spaced $3.1$ hours apart (quarter period $= 12.4/4 = 3.1$); $D$-coordinates: midline ($8$), minimum ($5$), midline ($8$), maximum ($11$), midline ($8$); all five coordinates required
parts.scoring_notes: Multiple valid parameterizations exist. With $D(t) = -3\sin(\pi t/6.2) + 8$: at $t=0$, $D = 8$ (midline); at $t = 3.1$, $D = 5$ (minimum); at $t = 6.2$, $D = 8$ (midline); at $t = 9.3$, $D = 11$ (maximum); at $t = 12.4$, $D = 8$ (midline). For part (ii), fractions are acceptable: quarter period $= 31/10 = 3.1$. Negative $t$-values acceptable. Partial credit if all $D$-coordinates are correct but $t$-spacing is wrong, or vice versa.

parts.prompt:
(i) Find all values of $t$ in $0 \leq t < 12.4$ at which the water depth is exactly $6.5$ meters. Express your answers as exact values.

(ii) On the interval $0 < t < 3.1$ (from midline-descending to minimum), describe the sign of $D(t) - 8$ and describe the concavity of the graph of $D$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct solutions — $-3\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) + 8 = 6.5 \Rightarrow \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) = \frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow \frac{\pi}{6.2}t = \frac{\pi}{6}$ or $\frac{5\pi}{6}$; so $t = \frac{6.2}{6} = \frac{31}{30}$ and $t = \frac{6.2 \cdot 5}{6} = \frac{31}{6}$; exact values $t = \frac{31}{30}$ and $t = \frac{31}{6}$; both required with no incorrect values

1 pt: Both descriptions correct — $D(t) - 8 < 0$ on $(0, 3.1)$ because $D < 8$ (depth is below midline, as the function is decreasing from 8 to 5) AND the graph of $D$ is concave up on $(0, 3.1)$ (the sinusoidal function $-3\sin(bt)$ on this interval is in the downward half but is concave up, meaning the rate of decrease is slowing as it approaches the minimum)
parts.scoring_notes: For part (i), exact fractions $31/30 \approx 1.033$ and $31/6 \approx 5.167$ are required. Decimal approximations are acceptable if derived from exact work. For part (ii), $D - 8 = -3\sin(bt) < 0$ on $(0, 3.1)$ since $\sin(bt) > 0$ there. The concavity of $-3\sin(bt)$ is $-3 \cdot (-\sin) = +\sin > 0$ on this interval (second derivative of sine is $-\sin$, so second derivative of $-3\sin$ is $+3\sin$, which is positive), hence concave up.

parts.prompt: A large cargo ship requires a minimum water depth of $9.5$ meters to safely enter the harbor. Determine the total number of hours per tidal cycle (period $= 12.4$ hours) during which the cargo ship can safely enter the harbor. Express your answer as an exact value.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct setup — $-3\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) + 8 \geq 9.5 \Rightarrow -3\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) \geq 1.5 \Rightarrow \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6.2}t\right) \leq -\frac{1}{2}$; the sine function is $\leq -1/2$ on the sub-interval corresponding to angles in $[\pi + \pi/6, 2\pi - \pi/6] = [7\pi/6, 11\pi/6]$

1 pt: Correct total time — the sine is $\leq -1/2$ for $\frac{2}{3}$ of a full period (from $7\pi/6$ to $11\pi/6$ spans $4\pi/6 \cdot \frac{1}{2\pi} = \frac{2\pi/3}{2\pi} = \frac{1}{3}$ of the period); so total safe time $= \frac{1}{3} \times 12.4 = \frac{12.4}{3} = \frac{62}{15}$ hours (accept decimal $\approx 4.133$ hours)
parts.scoring_notes: The safe entry window is when $D \geq 9.5$, meaning $\sin(bt) \leq -1/2$. The sine is $\leq -1/2$ for the interval from $7\pi/6$ to $11\pi/6$ within one period ($[0, 2\pi]$), which is a span of $4\pi/6 = 2\pi/3$ radians — that's $1/3$ of a full period. So the ship can enter for $12.4/3 = 62/15 \approx 4.13$ hours per tidal cycle. Exact answer $62/15$ hours earns full credit; decimal approximation is also acceptable with work.
